title: [DO NOT SUBMIT] [SCFToCalyx] [StaticLogicToCalyx] Initial separation of FuncOpPartialLoweringPattern.
body:
So I'm currently trying to pull out FuncOpPartialLoweringPattern and BuildOpGroups.
Since FuncOpPartialLoweringPattern contains the ProgramLoweringState, we need to add a template to it. This means we'll need to do the same thing for its child class `BuildOpGroups` as well when pulling it out. Result: all definitions would be in the header file.

Alternatives:
-  We could do template specialization in the .cpp as well, though I'm not sure that'd work in this case?
- Avoid templates by making the ComponentLoweringState a virtual class, and now each pass would be required to define all virtual methods. This would mean some class may have a bunch of `void Foo() { llvm_unreachable("not applicable"); }`.

Looking for some feedback before I commit to this refactoring.

Continued work on #2988 .
